question: Can orient.py be decomposed to <20KB while maintaining identical output?
method: DI extraction pattern (L-941): move display sections to orient_sections.py, utility functions to orient_state.py. Keep thin wrappers for backwards compat.
expect: orient.py drops from 40KB to ~15KB. main() becomes ~50-line coordinator.
actual: orient.py 40KB→13KB (70% reduction). 916→367 lines. main() is ~75 lines (coordinator + imports). All 8 tests pass. Output format identical.
diff: Predicted ~15KB, got 13KB (better). Predicted ~50-line main(), got ~75 lines (imports take space). Thin wrappers for backwards compat add ~80 lines that could be eliminated if tests updated.
